title: 学了1000天还是开不了口？语言APP的陷阱与突破

youtube.publish_title: Why You Still Can't Speak Chinese After 1000 Days on Duolingo | 为什么学了很久还是不会说

chapters.title: 连续打卡一千天的错觉
做选择题和真实聊天的区别

chapters.title_en: The Translation Trap in Your Brain
chapters.title: 脑子里的“翻译机”陷阱

chapters.title_en: Fear of Making Mistakes (The Heart System)
chapters.title: 害怕犯错：被软件的“红心”绑架

chapters.title_en: How to Start Speaking Real Chinese
chapters.title: 如何打破瓶颈，真正开口说

chapters.title_en: Conclusion: Apps are Just the Starting Line
chapters.title: 总结：软件只是起点，不是终点

in_this_episode_you_will_learn: Why maintaining a long streak on language apps doesn't guarantee speaking fluency.
The difference between passive vocabulary and active vocabulary in real-life conversations.
How to overcome the fear of making mistakes when speaking Chinese.
Practical tips for transitioning from app-based learning to real human communication.
Useful HSK3 vocabulary and expressions for discussing language learning.
description_intro: Have you been using language learning apps like Duolingo for months or even years, but still freeze up when trying to have a real conversation? You are not alone! In today's episode, Panpan and Lao Lu discuss the common trap of app-based language learning.

We dive into the psychological reasons why gamified apps make us feel like we are progressing, even when our speaking skills remain stagnant. From the illusion of multiple-choice questions to the fear of losing 'hearts', we explore why real life doesn't work like an app.

Tune in to hear our personal experiences with language learning, and get practical, actionable advice on how to build a 'thick skin', stop translating in your head, and finally start speaking Chinese with confidence.

blocks.purpose: 开场问候，通过盼盼朋友的具体故事引入今天的主题

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 大家好，欢迎来到我们的中文日常播客。我是盼盼，今天也陪大家一起轻松听中文。
segments.en: Hello everyone, welcome to our daily Chinese podcast. I'm Panpan, here to help you listen to Chinese in a relaxed way today.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 大家好，我是老路。盼盼，今天你想聊点什么？
segments.en: Hello everyone, I'm Lao Lu. Panpan, what do you want to talk about today?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 今天这个话题啊，跟我昨天遇到的一件小事有关。你们有没有用过手机上那些学语言的软件？比如一只绿色的猫头鹰。
segments.en: Today's topic is related to a little thing I encountered yesterday. Have you guys ever used those language learning apps on your phone? Like a certain green owl.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 昨天我看到我的一个外国朋友，他的手机屏幕上显示，他学中文已经连续打卡一千天了！
segments.en: Yesterday I saw a foreign friend of mine, and his phone screen showed that he had maintained a Chinese learning streak for a thousand days!

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哇，一千天！那也就是差不多三年的时间每天都在学，他的中文一定说得特别流利了吧？
segments.en: Wow, a thousand days! That means studying every day for almost three years. His Chinese must be spoken very fluently by now, right?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 问题就出在这里，路哥。我当时觉得很佩服，就马上用中文跟他说：“哇，你学了这么久，那我们用中文聊聊天吧？”
segments.en: That's exactly the problem, Brother Lu. I was really impressed at the time, so I immediately said to him in Chinese, 'Wow, you've been learning for so long, let's chat in Chinese?'

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 结果你猜怎么着？他突然变得特别紧张，脸都红了。憋了半天，只结结巴巴地说了一句：“你好，我喜欢吃苹果。”然后就切回英文了。
segments.en: And guess what? He suddenly became very nervous and his face turned red. He held it in for a long time and only stuttered out, 'Hello, I like eating apples.' And then he switched back to English.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 连续学了一千天，但还是不知道怎么开口聊天。其实，我觉得这不仅仅是他一个人的问题，很多用软件学外语的人都有这个困扰。
segments.en: Learning continuously for a thousand days, but still not knowing how to start a conversation. Actually, I think this is not just his problem alone; many people who use apps to learn foreign languages have this struggle.

blocks.purpose: 探讨打卡游戏化带来的“进步错觉”

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 现在很多手机软件做得特别像游戏。你每天上去做几道题，它就给你一些积分，或者给你一个奖励，这叫作“游戏化”。
segments.en: Nowadays, many mobile apps are designed exactly like games. You go on every day to answer a few questions, and it gives you some points or a reward. This is called 'gamification'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对，这种感觉太让人上瘾了！为了不让那个“连续打卡”的记录断掉，很多人每天晚上睡觉前，就算很困，也要拿起手机随便点两下。
segments.en: Yes, this feeling is so addictive! In order not to break that 'streak' record, many people will pick up their phones and casually tap a few times before going to bed every night, even if they are very sleepy.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我觉得到后来，大家的重点已经不是“学语言”了，而是“玩游戏得高分”。
segments.en: I think later on, people's focus is no longer on 'learning a language', but on 'playing a game to get a high score'.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这就是问题所在。当你的注意力全在赚取积分和保持记录上的时候，你其实会产生一种错觉，觉得自己的水平在不断提高。
segments.en: That is where the problem lies. When your attention is entirely on earning points and keeping the record, you actually develop an illusion, thinking your level is constantly improving.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 进步的错觉……你这么一说，确实是这样。因为软件每天都在夸你，告诉你“你太棒了”、“你击败了百分之九十的人”。
segments.en: The illusion of progress... now that you mention it, it really is like that. Because the app praises you every day, telling you 'You are awesome' or 'You beat 90% of people'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 但其实，现实生活中没有人会因为你说对了一个简单的词就给你放烟花。
segments.en: But in reality, no one in real life is going to set off fireworks for you just because you said a simple word correctly.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错，软件里的成功很容易得到，但在真实的咖啡馆里去用中文点一杯咖啡，挑战要大得多。这也难怪你的朋友一千天了还是不敢开口。
segments.en: Exactly, success inside the app is easy to get, but going into a real cafe to order a coffee in Chinese is a much bigger challenge. It's no wonder your friend still doesn't dare to speak after a thousand days.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 那路哥，到底为什么会这样呢？明明他认识很多汉字，也知道很多词语的意思，为什么一到说话脑子就卡住了呢？
segments.en: So Brother Lu, why on earth does this happen? He clearly recognizes many Chinese characters and knows the meanings of many words, so why does his brain get stuck the moment he tries to speak?

blocks.purpose: 盼盼分享个人经验，区分“输入”和“输出”的不同

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这其实跟我们在软件上学习的方式有关。在回答这个问题之前，盼盼，你自己学外语的时候有过类似的经历吗？
segments.en: This is actually related to the way we learn on the apps. Before answering this question, Panpan, have you had a similar experience when learning a foreign language yourself?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 太有了！我以前用手机软件自学过一阵子西班牙语。当时我觉得自己学得可好了，每天都能满分过关。
segments.en: Absolutely! I used a mobile app to teach myself Spanish for a while in the past. At the time, I felt I was learning so well and could pass with a perfect score every day.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 软件上经常出现这种题：上面是一句话，下面给你四个选项。只要看到关键词，我就能猜出正确答案。比如看到“吃”的单词，我就选跟“食物”有关的选项。
segments.en: This kind of question often appeared on the app: a sentence at the top, and four options below. As long as I saw the keyword, I could guess the correct answer. For example, if I saw the word for 'eat', I would choose the option related to 'food'.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 也就是说，你其实是在做一种“连线游戏”，对吧？
segments.en: In other words, you were actually playing a kind of 'matching game', right?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对，就是找规律！后来我真的去西班牙旅行的时候，想去餐厅点菜，服务员站在我面前看着我，我瞬间就崩溃了。
segments.en: Yes, just looking for patterns! Later, when I actually traveled to Spain and wanted to order food in a restaurant, the waiter stood in front of me looking at me, and I instantly broke down.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 因为服务员没有给我四个选项让我选啊！
segments.en: Because the waiter didn't give me four options to choose from!

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈，是的，现实生活中是没有选择题的。你刚才说的这段经历，完美地解释了“被动词汇”和“主动词汇”的区别。
segments.en: Haha, yes, there are no multiple-choice questions in real life. The experience you just shared perfectly explains the difference between 'passive vocabulary' and 'active vocabulary'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 被动和主动？路哥，这两个词怎么理解？
segments.en: Passive and active? Brother Lu, how should we understand these two terms?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 被动词汇就是，你看到它、听到它，你能认出来，这就叫输入。但在绝大多数语言软件里，你做的事情90%都是被动认字。
segments.en: Passive vocabulary is when you see it or hear it, you can recognize it; this is called input. But in the vast majority of language apps, 90% of what you do is passively recognizing words.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 而主动词汇是，当你的脑子里有一个想法，你能自己把它找出来，并用嘴说出来，这叫作输出。
segments.en: Active vocabulary, on the other hand, is when you have an idea in your head, you can retrieve it yourself and speak it out with your mouth. This is called output.

blocks.purpose: 引入成语“活学活用”，并解释现实对话的不可预测性

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我明白了。也就是说，我的那个外国朋友，他的一千天可能只是锻炼了“眼睛看”的能力，但并没有锻炼“嘴巴说”的能力。
segments.en: I get it. That means my foreign friend's one thousand days might have only trained his 'eye reading' ability, but didn't train his 'mouth speaking' ability.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 完全正确。在软件里，所有的句子都是设计好的，场景是非常固定的。但真实的聊天是不可预测的。
segments.en: Absolutely correct. In the app, all the sentences are pre-designed, and the scenarios are very fixed. But real conversation is unpredictable.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这里我想教大家一个很有用的成语，叫做“活学活用”。
segments.en: Here I want to teach everyone a very useful idiom, called 'huo xue huo yong' (learn flexibly and use practically).

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 活学活用？活是生活的活，学是学习的学，对吗？
segments.en: Huo xue huo yong? 'Huo' as in life (alive), and 'xue' as in learn, right?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对。“活”在这里的意思是灵活。这个成语的意思是，你学到了知识，不能死死地记在脑子里，而是要根据实际情况灵活地去使用它。
segments.en: Right. 'Huo' here means flexible. This idiom means that when you learn knowledge, you shouldn't just memorize it rigidly in your head, but rather use it flexibly according to the actual situation.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 可是，如果在软件上只能选A、B、C、D，确实很难练习“活学活用”。因为你没有机会自己创造句子啊。
segments.en: But if you can only choose A, B, C, or D on the app, it's indeed very hard to practice 'learning and using flexibly'. Because you don't have the opportunity to create sentences yourself.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是这样的。你朋友可能在软件里背过一句话叫“这杯咖啡多少钱”。但在真正的咖啡馆里，老板可能会问他：“你要大杯还是小杯？热的还是冰的？要加糖吗？”
segments.en: That's how it is. Your friend might have memorized a sentence in the app like 'How much is this cup of coffee?'. But in a real cafe, the boss might ask him: 'Do you want a large or small cup? Hot or iced? Do you want sugar?'

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哈哈，如果他只背了那一句，这时候肯定就傻眼了，完全听不懂老板在说什么，更不知道怎么回答。
segments.en: Haha, if he only memorized that one sentence, he would definitely be dumbfounded at this point, completely not understanding what the boss is saying, let alone knowing how to answer.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 所以，真正的语言能力不仅仅是词汇量，更是面对未知情况时的反应能力，也就是如何“活学活用”。
segments.en: Therefore, true language ability is not just about vocabulary size, but also the ability to react when facing unknown situations, which is how to 'learn and use flexibly'.

blocks.purpose: 讨论母语翻译习惯如何拖慢语速

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 除了做选择题这个习惯，我发现还有一个很大的陷阱。也是我自己遇到过的，那就是在脑子里当“翻译机”。
segments.en: Besides the habit of doing multiple-choice questions, I found there's another huge trap. It's also something I've encountered myself, which is acting as a 'translation machine' in your head.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哦？翻译机陷阱，这个说法很有意思，你具体解释一下。
segments.en: Oh? The translation machine trap, that's an interesting way to put it, please explain it in detail.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 就是很多初学者，包括我自己，在说话之前，脑子里会先用自己的母语想好句子，然后再一个词一个词地翻译成外语。
segments.en: It means many beginners, including myself, will first formulate the sentence in our native language in our heads before speaking, and then translate it into the foreign language word by word.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 比如我想说“我明天要去超市”。我会先在脑子里想英文 'I will go to the supermarket tomorrow'。然后我再想，'I' 是我，'will' 怎么说来着？哦，是会，或者要。
segments.en: For example, if I want to say 'I'm going to the supermarket tomorrow'. I'll first think of the English in my head 'I will go to the supermarket tomorrow'. Then I think again, 'I' is wo, how do I say 'will'? Oh, it's hui, or yao.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 等我把每一个词的中文意思找出来，再排好顺序，可能一分钟都过去了。对方早就不想听了。
segments.en: By the time I find the Chinese meaning for every single word and put them in order, maybe a whole minute has passed. The other person would have lost interest in listening long ago.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的，这是一个非常典型的过程。语言软件其实强化了这种翻译习惯。因为你在软件上做的练习，大部分就是让你把英文句子翻译成中文，或者把中文翻译成英文。
segments.en: Yes, this is a very typical process. Language apps actually reinforce this translation habit. Because most of the exercises you do on the app involve translating an English sentence into Chinese, or translating Chinese into English.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 长期这样训练，你的大脑就会习惯性地依赖你的母语作为一座桥梁。
segments.en: If you train like this for a long time, your brain will habitually rely on your native language as a bridge.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对！可是真实的交流速度太快了，根本没有时间让你走这座桥。等你翻译完，话题都已经换到下一个了。
segments.en: Right! But the speed of real communication is too fast; there's simply no time for you to walk across this bridge. By the time you finish translating, the topic has already moved on to the next one.

blocks.purpose: 老路解释如何通过“语块”来摆脱翻译思维

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 为了摆脱这个翻译机陷阱，我们需要培养中文思维。不要一个词一个词去记，而是要记“语块”。
segments.en: To break free from this translation machine trap, we need to cultivate Chinese thinking. Don't memorize word by word, but rather memorize 'chunks'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 语块？就是几个词放在一起的一个短语吗？
segments.en: Chunks? Does that mean a phrase with a few words put together?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 对，或者叫固定的搭配。比如你看到一个好久不见的朋友，如果你按单词拼凑，可能会想“好”怎么说，“久”怎么说。
segments.en: Right, or fixed collocations. For example, if you see a friend you haven't seen for a long time, if you piece it together word by word, you might think how to say 'good' (hao), how to say 'long time' (jiu).

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但如果你把“好久不见”当作一个整体记住。下次见到朋友，你的大脑就会像条件反射一样，直接跳出这四个字，不需要经过母语翻译。
segments.en: But if you remember 'hao jiu bu jian' (long time no see) as a whole entity. The next time you see a friend, your brain will act like a conditioned reflex and pop out these four words directly, without needing to go through native language translation.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 啊，我懂了。就像我们说“你好”的时候，没有人会在脑子里翻译说，你就是 you，好就是 good。我们就是本能地脱口而出。
segments.en: Ah, I get it. Just like when we say 'ni hao' (hello), no one translates in their head saying 'ni' is you, 'hao' is good. We just blurt it out instinctively.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 其实这也是我自己走过的一个弯路。以前我学英语的时候，总是喜欢把每个介词都搞清楚。其实有些常用的话，直接背下来，说出来就行了。
segments.en: Actually, this is also a detour I took myself. When I learned English before, I always liked to figure out every single preposition. In reality, for some commonly used sentences, you just memorize them directly and say them.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是啊，交流的目的是为了传达意思，而不是做完美的语法分析。
segments.en: Yes, the purpose of communication is to convey meaning, not to do a perfect grammar analysis.

blocks.purpose: 讨论完美主义和害怕犯错的心理障碍

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 说到语法完美，我觉得这引出了另一个很大的心理障碍，就是害怕犯错。路哥，我又要吐槽软件的机制了。
segments.en: Speaking of grammatical perfection, I think this brings up another huge psychological barrier, which is the fear of making mistakes. Brother Lu, I'm going to complain about the app's mechanism again.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 哈哈，好啊，你尽情吐槽，我听听。
segments.en: Haha, alright, complain all you want, I'm listening.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 很多语言软件里有一个“红心”的设定。也就是代表你的生命值。如果你做错了一道题，它就会扣掉你一颗心，还会发出那种让人很有挫败感的“噔噔”的声音。
segments.en: Many language apps have a 'red heart' setting. Which represents your life points. If you get a question wrong, it deducts a heart from you, and even plays that very frustrating 'dun-dun' sound.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 等到心被扣光了，你就不能继续学习了，必须等几个小时或者花钱买。
segments.en: Once all your hearts are gone, you can't continue learning; you have to wait for a few hours or spend money to buy them.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 确实有这个设计，这其实是在惩罚错误。
segments.en: There is indeed this design; this is actually punishing mistakes.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对啊！潜移默化中，它在告诉我们：犯错是不好的，犯错是有代价的。为了保住红心，大家做题的时候就会变得小心翼翼。
segments.en: Exactly! Subtly, it's telling us: making mistakes is bad, and mistakes have a cost. In order to keep the red hearts, people become very cautious when answering questions.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这种习惯一旦带到现实生活里就惨了。我那个朋友跟我聊天的时候，我能感觉到他每说一个词都在思考语法对不对，生怕说错了被我扣分。
segments.en: Once this habit is brought into real life, it's a disaster. When my friend was chatting with me, I could feel that with every word he said, he was thinking about whether the grammar was right, terrified of making a mistake and losing points from me.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但是真正的生活里没有红心系统，即使说错了，对方一般也能猜到你的意思，而且绝对不会惩罚你。
segments.en: But there is no red heart system in real life. Even if you say it wrong, the other person can usually guess what you mean, and will definitely not punish you.

blocks.purpose: 老路引入“脸皮厚”的概念，鼓励放下包袱

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 关于害怕犯错，中国有一句很接地气的话，也是学语言的秘诀，那就是要“脸皮厚”。
segments.en: Regarding the fear of making mistakes, there is a very down-to-earth saying in China, which is also the secret to learning a language, and that is to be 'thick-skinned' (lian pi hou).

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 脸皮厚，哈哈！能不能给还在学中文的听众朋友解释一下什么叫脸皮厚？
segments.en: Thick-skinned, haha! Can you explain to our listeners who are still learning Chinese what it means to be thick-skinned?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 脸皮就是脸上的皮肤。脸皮厚，意思就是不怕丢脸，不在乎别人的看法，不会因为犯错而觉得尴尬。
segments.en: Lian pi is the skin on your face. Thick facial skin means not being afraid of losing face, not caring about what others think, and not feeling embarrassed because of making mistakes.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 如果你想要从能听懂变成能说出口，第一步就是放下完美主义，勇敢地做个脸皮厚的人。
segments.en: If you want to go from being able to understand to being able to speak, the first step is to let go of perfectionism and bravely be a thick-skinned person.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我太同意了。其实我们回想一下小孩子是怎么学说话的？小孩子说错话的时候，从来不会觉得害羞，他们就是不停地说。
segments.en: I completely agree. Actually, if we think back to how young children learn to speak? When children say something wrong, they never feel shy; they just keep talking.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 大人听懂了，给他们反馈，他们就慢慢调整对了。这也是语言学习最自然的过程。
segments.en: When adults understand and give them feedback, they slowly adjust to the right way. This is also the most natural process of language learning.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 没错。如果你因为害怕语法错误而选择闭嘴，那你就失去了获得真实反馈的机会。
segments.en: Exactly. If you choose to keep your mouth shut out of fear of grammar mistakes, then you lose the opportunity to get real feedback.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 就算你把主语谓语搞混了，或者量词用错了，比如把“一个人”说成了“一只人”，大家除了觉得有点可爱之外，完全能听懂，沟通的目的依然达到了。
segments.en: Even if you mix up the subject and predicate, or use the wrong measure word, for example saying 'yi zhi ren' instead of 'yi ge ren' (one person), besides thinking it's a bit cute, people can completely understand you, and the goal of communication is still achieved.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 哈哈，一只人，路哥你这个例子太生动了。的确，生活里哪怕语法错了，只要能把想表达的事情说清楚，那就是成功的交流。
segments.en: Haha, one animal-person, your example is so vivid, Brother Lu. Indeed, in real life, even if the grammar is wrong, as long as you can clearly say what you want to express, that is successful communication.

blocks.purpose: 盼盼提出实际建议：通过“跟读”（Shadowing）来练习嘴部肌肉

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那我们分析了这么多原因，接下来总该给大家一些实用的建议了。如果已经学了很久，想要打破这个不敢开口的瓶颈，我们该怎么做呢？
segments.en: Since we've analyzed so many reasons, we should give everyone some practical advice next. If you've been learning for a long time and want to break through this bottleneck of not daring to speak, what should we do?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我先来分享一个我自己觉得特别管用的方法。虽然听起来有点傻，但真的很有用，那就是“跟读”。
segments.en: Let me first share a method that I personally find very effective. Even though it sounds a bit silly, it's really useful, and that is 'shadowing' (gen du).

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 跟读，也就是跟着原声一起大声读出来，对吧？
segments.en: Shadowing, which means reading out loud along with the original audio, right?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对的。很多人听播客或者看视频的时候，习惯默默地在心里翻译。我现在建议大家，不仅要听，还要动起嘴唇，模仿母语者的发音、语调甚至情绪。
segments.en: Yes. When many people listen to podcasts or watch videos, they are used to translating silently in their hearts. Now I suggest everyone not only to listen but also to move your lips, imitating the native speaker's pronunciation, intonation, and even emotions.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 比如大家现在听着我们的播客，如果你在一个人散步，就可以跟着我刚才说的话，小声重复一遍。
segments.en: For example, as everyone is listening to our podcast right now, if you are walking alone, you can softly repeat what I just said.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这个方法确实很好。说话其实也是一种肌肉记忆。长期只用眼睛看的人，口腔肌肉是不适应说中文的。跟读可以帮你热身。
segments.en: This method is indeed very good. Speaking is actually also a form of muscle memory. For people who only use their eyes to read for a long time, their mouth muscles are not adapted to speaking Chinese. Shadowing can help you warm up.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 是的，很多时候你会发现，一句话你明明认识所有的字，但就是说得很结巴，这就是因为嘴巴没有形成记忆。通过跟读，把舌头练顺了，真的要开口时就不会那么紧张了。
segments.en: Yes, many times you will find that even though you clearly recognize all the words in a sentence, you just stutter when saying it. This is because the mouth hasn't formed the memory. By shadowing and smoothing out your tongue, you won't be so nervous when you really need to speak.

blocks.purpose: 老路建议寻找真实的语伴，强调真实交流的重要性

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 除了自己练习跟读，我的建议是，一定要去寻找跟真人对话的机会。去感受那种没有字幕、没有四个选项的真实交流。
segments.en: Besides practicing shadowing by yourself, my advice is that you must go look for opportunities to converse with real people. Go feel that real communication without subtitles and without four options.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 路哥，但对很多人来说，找不到中国人聊天怎么办呢？
segments.en: Brother Lu, but for many people, what should they do if they can't find Chinese people to chat with?

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 现在互联网这么发达，找语伴其实并不难。有很多专门的语言交换应用，你可以教别人英语，别人教你中文。
segments.en: The internet is so developed now; finding a language partner is actually not hard. There are many dedicated language exchange apps where you can teach someone English, and they teach you Chinese.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 或者哪怕是在网上找一个老师，每周聊半个小时的天。关键是要把自己放在一个“必须输出”的环境里。
segments.en: Or even just finding a teacher online and chatting for half an hour a week. The key is to put yourself in an environment where you 'must output'.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 这就像学游泳一样，你在岸上看再多的教学视频，在软件上做再多的选择题，如果不敢跳进水里，永远也学不会游泳。
segments.en: This is just like learning to swim. No matter how many tutorial videos you watch on the shore, or how many multiple-choice questions you do on the app, if you don't dare to jump into the water, you will never learn how to swim.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 这个比喻非常贴切。真实对话就是把你扔进水里，刚开始你可能会呛水，会说错话，会听不懂，但挣扎几次之后，你就会慢慢找到感觉了。
segments.en: This metaphor is very apt. Real conversation is throwing you into the water. At first, you might swallow some water, say the wrong things, or not understand, but after struggling a few times, you will slowly get the hang of it.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 对，而且和真人聊天最有趣的地方在于，它有情感交流。当你用不流利的中文逗笑了一个中国朋友，那种成就感，是软件上的积分永远给不了的。
segments.en: Yes, and the most interesting part of chatting with a real person is that there is emotional exchange. When you make a Chinese friend laugh with your not-so-fluent Chinese, that sense of achievement is something app points can never give you.

blocks.purpose: 全面总结：理性看待语言软件的价值

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 那么，说到底，用软件打卡学中文到底有没有用呢？
segments.en: So, when it comes down to it, is it useful at all to use apps to keep a streak and learn Chinese?

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 我觉得肯定是有用的，尤其是在刚开始的阶段。它帮我们认识了汉字，积累了词汇，而且每天打卡确实能帮我们建立学习的习惯。
segments.en: I think it is definitely useful, especially at the very beginning stages. It helps us recognize Chinese characters, accumulate vocabulary, and daily check-ins really do help us build a learning habit.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 是的，我们并不是要否定这些工具的价值。语言软件就像是一个起跑线，它带你进入了中文的世界。
segments.en: Yes, we are not denying the value of these tools. Language apps are like a starting line; they bring you into the Chinese world.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 但是大家一定要明白，软件只是起点，而不是终点。当你的词汇量积累到一定程度时，你就必须主动跨出那一步。
segments.en: But everyone must understand that the app is just the starting point, not the finish line. When your vocabulary has accumulated to a certain level, you must take the initiative to step out.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 没错，不要让自己永远留在那座舒适的“选择题城堡”里。要把背过的词，活学活用到真实的生活中去。
segments.en: Exactly, don't let yourself stay forever in that comfortable 'multiple-choice castle'. You have to flexibly use the words you've memorized into real life.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 厚着脸皮去说，去犯错，然后去纠正，这样你的中文才能真正变得有生命力。
segments.en: Be thick-skinned to speak, to make mistakes, and then to correct them. Only then can your Chinese truly become lively.

blocks.purpose: 感谢收听，鼓励互动，引导订阅和点赞

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 好啦，这就是我们今天关于语言学习软件和如何开口说中文的讨论。希望能给还在苦苦坚持打卡、却不敢开口的你一点点启发和勇气。
segments.en: Alright, this is our discussion today about language learning apps and how to open your mouth to speak Chinese. We hope it gives a little inspiration and courage to those of you who are struggling to keep your streak but are afraid to speak.

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 大家也可以在评论区分享一下，你用软件学中文坚持了多少天？遇到过想要开口却卡住的情况吗？我们很想听听你们的故事。
segments.en: Everyone is also welcome to share in the comments section: how many days have you kept up learning Chinese with an app? Have you ever encountered a situation where you wanted to speak but got stuck? We really want to hear your stories.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 不要害怕犯错，勇敢地在评论区用中文给我们留言吧！如果你喜欢今天的播客，别忘了给我们点赞、订阅我们的频道。
segments.en: Don't be afraid of making mistakes, bravely leave us a comment in Chinese in the comments section! If you liked today's podcast, don't forget to like this video and subscribe to our channel.

segments.speaker: female
segments.text: 你们的支持是我们持续更新的动力。那么感谢大家的陪伴，我们下次再见啦！
segments.en: Your support is the motivation for us to keep updating. So thank you everyone for your company, we'll see you next time!

segments.speaker: male
segments.text: 感谢收听，祝大家中文越说越溜。下次见！
segments.en: Thanks for listening, wishing everyone's Chinese gets more and more fluent. See you next time!
